title: [CI:DOCS] VOLUME must be declared after RUN chown command
body:
Podman and Docker will not commit changes via RUN command
of a VOLUME directory, so we need to chown path first.

Not doing do will cause: https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2009266

Signed-off-by: Jindrich Novy <jnovy@redhat.com>
Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

Replaces https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/11952

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *lsm5*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Any chance you can add a comment on the VOLUME directives indicating why they can't be moved, so we don't re-break this in the future?

I like @mheon's thought

It would be also nice for the build process to at least note the ordering as being problematic.  Granted this fixes our issue, but someone else might stumble upon this in the future.
I'm going to merge this to make progress for the RHEL builds.  I'll circle back tomorrow and will make the touch ups @mheon requested.
@TomSweeneyRedHat I added  a note about volumes.
